free_text: Cross sections of deuteron-induced reactions on natSb
High purity thin Sb targets of natural isotopic
Sb targets with natural isotopic composition were
vacuum evaporated onto kapton backinb. 20 sandwich
average thickness of 1.4 mg/cm2. The kapton foil
acted also as catcher foil of the recoiled reaction
products. The beam energy was 50 MeV, the incident
deuteron beam was about 100 nA, irradiation time was
1.5 h. The beam was monitored by Al and Ti monitor
The initial beam uncertainty was +-0.3 MeV.
The uncertainty of the beam energy throughout
the stack increases to 1.6 MeV at the last foil.
The uncertainty of the cross-sections were
estimated taking the independent relative errors of the
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS-2010.
